reforms.description: A new law for the quality and development of tertiary education was approved, merging responsibilities for science and technology with higher education. This is part of a broader programme to strengthen competitiveness and efficiency, promoting investment, exports, competition, deregulation, and innovation, including the introduction of a Productivity and Growth Programme aimed at enhancing competition and improving the business environment.
reforms.component_name: Tertiary Education Law and Competitiveness Programme
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance the quality of education and promote a competitive business environment, which are essential for long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was approved and the programme was initiated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in the education and innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The new government’s strategy to strengthen competitiveness and efficiency in order to achieve higher long-term growth is set out in a broadly-based Productivity and Growth Programme, presented in June 2002.

reforms.description: A new tax incentive system allows firms to deduct a significant percentage of eligible R&D expenditures from their taxable income, aimed at encouraging private sector investment in research. This is complemented by the introduction of a more favorable R&D tax regime and the creation of an Investment Tax Credit to replace previous incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives are designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new tax incentive system has been put into effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major overhaul of the tax incentives for R&D, significantly impacting private sector investment.
reforms.source_quote: Steps taken to promote science and technology include increasing R&D spending and fostering collaboration between universities and enterprises.

reforms.description: Recent reforms have been introduced to simplify administrative procedures and shorten delays in starting up new enterprises, essential for fostering innovation and the creation of new firms. Additionally, a public venture capital syndication fund has been created to support the development of R&D activities, while the Operational Programme for the Economy aims to provide financial and technical support for developing an entrepreneurial culture, particularly in ICT sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms are aimed at enhancing the startup ecosystem, which is vital for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly improve the business environment for startups but do not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Operational Programme for the Economy, 2000-06, aims at providing financial and technical support for the development of an entrepreneurial culture by supporting investment projects in ICT sectors.

reforms.description: The Operational Programme for Science, Technology and Innovation 2000-06 aims to develop the science and technology base through funding programmes for advanced training in R&D and support for R&D institutions. Additionally, the government initiated a rationalisation and reorientation of public laboratories towards applied research to better serve the business community, alongside the launch of an Integrated Innovation Support Programme aimed at enhancing innovation across various sectors.
reforms.component_name: Operational Programme for Science and Technology
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to strengthen the science and technology base, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in research infrastructure but is not a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Operational Programme for Science, Technology and Innovation 2000-06, prepared under the aegis of the EC, addresses these two issues in a complementary way.
